characters.name: Home
characters.description: Welcome to the Highway to Heaven Wiki ! A probationary angel, sent back to Earth, teams with an ex-cop to help people.

characters.name: Mark Gordon
characters.description: Mark GordonRelativesScotty Wilson - cousin's husbandDiane Wilson (nee Gordon) - cousinLeslie Gordon - brotherBiographical informationMarital statusWidowedDate of birth1930sPlace of birthOaklandPhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHeight6' 1"Eye colorDark BrownAn ex-cop from Oakland. He was kicked off the police force for using excessive force. He meets Jonathan Smith, and they become best friends and partners. Mark is a cynic, but deeply cares for people once he gets to know them. A recurring theme is when he says anything that relates to Hell, something bad happens to him. He especially loves fishing and eating. Diane Wilson (nee Gordon) - cousinLeslie Gordon - brother He is introduced in the episode "Pilot, Part 1," where he was living with his sister, and was an alcoholic who was fired from another job for fighting. He becomes suspicious of Jonathan, and he breaks into his apartment to find nothing, and Jonathan is forced to reveal to him that he's an angel.

characters.name: Michael Landon
characters.description: Michael LandonAliasesEugene Maurice OrowitzBiographical informationDate of birthOctober 31, 1936Place of birthQueens, New YorkDate of deathJuly 1, 1991Place of deathMalibu, CAPhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHeight5'9Eye colorHazelMichael Landon was born Eugene Maurice Orowitz in the Forest Hills area of Queens, New York on October 31, 1936.  An athlete as a young man, Michael was accepted to USC on a Track & Field scholarship, particularly for javelin throwing.  A shoulder injury deprived him of both a sports career and his scholarship, so he quit school to work odd jobs until he decided that acting was the life for him.  With a change of name (taken directly out of the phone book), he had a couple big roles inI Was a Teenage WerewolfandThe Legend of Tom Dooley, but the role that made him a household name was for a new western show in 1959 calledBonanzaon which he playedLittle Joe Cartwright.Bonanzalasted 14 seasons, with Landon moving into the directing and writing arenas on more than a fewBonanzaepisodes.Bonanzawas canceled in 1973, but Landon did not have to wait long for his next role,Charles "Pa" Ingalls, in the new series,Little House on the Prairie.  That show lasted 8 seasons and gave Landon a healthy resume for writing, directing, and acting.  It didn't hurt that the show was very popular with women, particularly those who enjoyed viewing Landon frequently without a shirt and, as Alison Arngrim (who playedNellie Oleson) stated in her autobiography,Confessions of a Prairie Bitch, his "commando" pants-wearing habits.  FollowingPrairie, Landon jumped intoHighway to Heavenin 1984, allowing him to work 5 more seasons with hisPrairieco-star,Victor French. Landon only did a couple more TV movies onceHighway to Heavenwas canceled in 1989 before he was diagnosed with terminal pancreatic and liver cancer.  He passed away on July 1, 1991 in Malibu, CA, at the age of 54.  For a full list of credits, visit Landon's IMDB page.[1] Michael Landon was know for playing tricks on the set of many of his shows and movies, especially with Victor French. They once stole a golf cart and drove it around for an hour before a producer saw them and stopped them.

characters.name: Scotty Wilson
characters.description: Scotty Wilson is a quadriplegic lawyer who has appeared in many episodes of Highway to Heaven. The first episode he appears in is One Fresh Batch of Lemonade. He also has appeared in A Match Made In Heaven, where he met and married Mark's cousin, Diane Gordon. Scotty was doing an audit for her former boss when she mentioned how her plans for a building where stolen while having dinner with Mark and Jonathan. Scotty helped her get her job back and they fell in love soon afterward. But after being in an accident involving a drunk driver, Scotty begins to have second thoughts and calls the wedding off. Jonathan, however, brings Scotty along when he visits an old wheelchair-bound friend who's married with kids. The visit sways Scotty, and he proposes to Diane soon afterward. They are married at the end of the episode. Scotty is also in The Monster, part 1 and part 2. He and Diane are having marital trouble, and Scotty is in denial. He is having trouble finding any clients. However, when the town's " boogeyman", a man named Julian with a large birthmark on his face, is accused of attempted murder of Rachel McCullough, a blind girl he fell in love with. Scotty tries Julian's case and wins. Scotty also accepts that he needs to try harder to get more clients and more cases, to overcome the fact that he's handicapped. Scotty and Diane make another appearance in A Special Love, part 1 and 2. Diane and Scotty find out that they are unable to have children, and Scotty refuses to adopt. Meanwhile, Jonathan needs Scotty's help to persuade a young boy named Tod, who is mentally impaired, to compete in Special Olympics. Scotty and Diane fall in love with Tod, who is in foster care, and Scotty changes his mind about adopting. However, Tod's parents never actually gave him up, which means without their consent, the Wilsons could not adopt him. Tod's parents have a restraining order put on Scotty and Diane, barring them from seeing Tod. Scotty then finds a way that Tod can separate from his parents so Scotty and Diane can adopt him. Eventually, once Tod's birth parents see how much their son loves the Wilsons, they sign the agreement and Tod becomes Scotty and Diane's son. Scotty and Tod enjoy nature walks, and Scotty was also dead set on teaching Tod to read.

characters.name: Victor French
characters.description: Victor FrenchVictor French as Isaiah Edwards on Little House on the Prairie.Biographical informationDate of birthDecember 4, 1934Place of birthSanta Barbara, CADate of deathJune 15, 1989Place of deathLos Angeles, CAPhysical descriptionSpeciesHumanGenderMaleHeight6'3Eye colorDark Brown Due to his gruff exterior, French often played bad guys in Westerns in which he also performed most stunts.  Born on December 4, 1934, French was the son of Ted French who was also an actor and stuntman in 1940's Westerns.  Victor French was a regular onGunsmokeplaying various roles during his 23 episodes.  It was in 1974 that he came to work withMichael LandononLittle House on the Prairiein the role ofIsaiah Edwards.  He leftLittle Housein 1977 to work on his own show, a sitcom calledCarter Country, but it only lasted two seasons.  He was welcomed back toLittle Houseby Landon in 1981 and he stayed until the show ended in 1984.  French was surprised that Landon was so agreeable to having him back on the show.  OnceLittle Houseended, French again teamed with Landon inHighway to Heavenin 1984.  A lifelong chain smoker, French passed away on June 15, 1989 from lung cancer shortly after filming the last episode ofHighway to Heaven.  His entire resume is available at IMDB.[1]
